mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following the unemployment threshold breach across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns during periods when the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, market participants anticipate aggressive central bank tightening to combat inflation, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 235.0 are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 70% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate demonstrate robust labor market absorption, boosting consumer spending capacity and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields a negative or zero average 60-day forward return over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues and earnings, mechanically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the 99.5 index level signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic activity, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of periods when industrial production falls below the 99.5 threshold.
